title: Eranthemum ciliatum
taxonomy: Eranthemum ciliatum is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This plant belongs to the order Lamiales. Further categorization places it within the genus Eranthemum. It is also known as the hairy lizard tail or ciliate lizard tail. The classification of Eranthemum ciliatum is often referred to as a flowering plant within the monocots division of the plant kingdom.
uses: Eranthemum ciliatum is a plant with various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Its extracts have also been shown to exhibit antioxidant and hepatoprotective activities, indicating potential benefits for liver health. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
benefits: Eranthemum ciliatum, also known as the hairy foxglove, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine in different cultures. intro: Eranthemum ciliatum, also known as the hairy foxglove or hairy eranthemum, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eranthemum ciliatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin infections.
